rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 1, 2007, LINE 5. DEFENDANTS CHARLES HURWITZ AND MAXXAM INC.'S MOTION For Change Of Venue And REQUEST For Judicial NOTICE: This motion is brought by an individual and corporation. The complaint alleges that the individual resides and has his principle place of business in Houston, Texas. The corporation is alleged to be a Delaware corporation with its principle place of business in Houston, Texas. There is no evidence that these defendants have submitted a statement designating the county in which either maintains its principle local office. As a consequence, the corporation may be sued in any county in California. Easton v. Superior Court (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 246-247. A corporation's motion to transfer can only be based on its principle place. Karson Industries, Inc. v. Superior Court (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 7, 8-9. It, therefore follows, that the motion cannot be based on the business of the other defendants, particularly in this case where said defendants have not appeared, have filed for bankruptcy and have obtained a stay. CC § 395 is the applicable venue provision based on the complaint's allegations. As to the moving defendants, the following language is applicable: "If none of the defendants reside in the state, ¿the action may be tried in the superior court in any county that the plaintiff may designate in his or her complaint." For the reasons stated, the motion is denied. The request for attorneys' fees is denied. =(302/PJM/CB-X)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY STEVENS VS. YOLANDA PAUL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike First, Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action As A Meritless Slapp Pursuant To Ccp 425.16;Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 1, 2007, LINE 9. DEFENDANT YOLANDA PAUL'S Special Motion To Strike First, Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action As A Meritless S.L.A.P.P. Pursuant To C.C.P. SECTION 425.16; Request For Judicial Notice: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THE FACT THAT THE COMPLAINT PRESENTS A PRIMA FACIE BASIS TO INVOKE THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED BY PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ESTABLISH A PRIMA FACIE CASE FOR THE CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED. THE ORDER IN THE FORCIBLE DETAINER ACTION, NUMBER 619215, FOUND THAT PLAINTIFF LACKED EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE STATEMENTS OF OTHERS. AS A CONSEQUENCE, PLAINTIFF'S ENTIRE CASE AGAINST DEFENDANT IS BASED ON THE 30-DAY NOTICE, BUT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED IN THIS ACTION THAT THE DEFENDANT LACKED TITLE TO THE PROPERTY. MOREOVER, SERVICE OF A 30-DAY NOTICE IS A PRIVILEGE ACT UNDER C.C. SECTION 47(B). =(302/PJM/KD)
